article.description: — OG Anunoby scored 25 points, Landry Shamet added 23 and the New York Knicks beat the Los Angeles Lakers 112-100 on Sunday night to spoil LeBron James' 32nd game at Madison Square Garden.
article.headline: Knicks beat Lakers 112-100 to spoil LeBron James' 32nd regular-season game at Madison Square Garden

images.caption: LeBron James quickly gets down the court to finish a lob from teammate Marcus Smart.
images.name: LeBron throws down a thunderous alley-oop slam

keywords: Los Angeles Lakers @ New York Knicks
article.linkText: Knicks beat Lakers 112-100 to spoil LeBron James' 32nd regular-season game at Madison Square Garden
article.story: NEW YORK -- — OG Anunoby scored 25 points, Landry Shamet added 23 and the New York Knicks beat the Los Angeles Lakers 112-100 on Sunday night to spoil LeBron James' 32nd game at Madison Square Garden. Josh Hart finished with 20 points and Jalen Brunson had 12 points and a season-high 13 assists for the Knicks, who matched a season high with their sixth straight win, most of them coming easily. James finished with 22 points, six assists and five rebounds after being chosen as a reserve earlier Sunday for his NBA-record 22nd consecutive All-Star selection. He fell to 23-9 in the regular season at MSG, where he came into the game having averaged 28.2 points, 7.6 assists and 7.0 rebounds. Luka Doncic had 30 points, 15 rebounds and eight assists. The game was exactly a year to the day of the Lakers’ last trip to New York, when shortly after their victory came word that they agreed to the blockbuster trade with Dallas that brought Doncic to Los Angeles. James hasn’t said if his 23rd season will be his last, but the possibility of a final MSG appearance sent ticket sales soaring, with seats far from the floor selling for more than $500 and ones near the court going for thousands of dollars. That, plus a nationally televised audience for the debut game of NBC’s “Sunday Night Basketball” schedule package, gave the game an even bigger feel that James’ usual trips. It was more competitive, too, as the Knicks late in his career are no longer the pushovers they were when his teams ruled the Eastern Conference. The Knicks outscored the Lakers 38-26 in the third quarter, getting three straight transition baskets and then closing with 3-pointers by Shamet, Brunson and Anunoby in a 15-5 surge that sent them to the fourth with a 90-82 lead. Karl-Anthony Towns had 11 points and 13 rebounds after learning he was an All-Star reserve. Up next Lakers: At Brooklyn on Tuesday night. Knicks: At Washington on Tuesday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/NBA

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder are back to being odds-on title favorites after taking a 3-2 series lead over the Spurs.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.
images.name: Knicks/Cavs [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) takes a shot as San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) defends during the first quarter during game two of the western conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Paycom Center.
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander how has even odds to win the Finals MVP.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander  [1296x729]

images.alt: VIctor Wembanyama

images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.caption: The Knicks have surged to +850 to win the NBA Finals following the first-round exit by the Boston Celtics.

images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.caption: The Lakers miss the perimeter shooting that Austin Reaves provides.
images.name: Austin Reaves [1296x729]

images.alt: Luka Doncic #77 of the Los Angeles Lakers handles the ball during the game against the Miami Heat on March 19, 2026 at Kaseya Center in Miami, Florida.
images.caption: The Lakers' championship odds are on the move again after Luka Doncic's injury.
images.name: Luka Doncic [1296x729]

images.alt: Los Angeles Lakers forward LeBron James (23) scores on a breakaway dunk during the first half of an NBA basketball game against the Orlando Magic, Saturday, March 21, 2026, in Orlando, Fla.
images.caption: LeBron James and the Lakers are on a roll as March winds down.
images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs handles the ball during the game against the Detroit Pistons on March 5, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jayson Tatum #0 of the Boston Celtics celebrates after scoring against the Dallas Mavericks during the second quarter at TD Garden on March 06, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics' title odds have shortened since Jayson Tatum's return last week.
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 and Devin Vassell #24 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the New Orleans Pelicans on January 25, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Led by Victor Wembanyama, the Spurs continue to see their NBA championship odds shorten.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Cade Cunningham #2 of the Detroit Pistons handles the ball during the game against the Denver Nuggets on February 3, 2026 at Little Caesars Arena in Detroit, Michigan.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
images.name: Jamal Murray  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers on December 29, 2025 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs have seen their odds to win the NBA championship shorten in the past week.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

videos.description: Los Angeles Lakers vs. New York Knicks: Game Highlights
videos.headline: Los Angeles Lakers vs. New York Knicks: Game Highlights

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Los Angeles Lakers vs. New York Knicks: Game Highlights) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: Jalen Brunson gets a 3-pointer to go from the corner.
videos.headline: Jalen Brunson nails an early Knicks 3

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Jalen Brunson nails an early Knicks 3) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: Marcus Smart draws the defense in and lobs it to Deandre Ayton for the Lakers basket.
videos.headline: Deandre Ayton rises for a two-handed alley-oop slam

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Deandre Ayton rises for a two-handed alley-oop slam) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: Luka Doncic hits his signature step-back 3-pointer for the Lakers.
videos.headline: Luka Doncic swishes a step-back 3

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Luka Doncic swishes a step-back 3) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: LeBron James spots up and drains a 3-pointer for the Lakers.
videos.headline: LeBron connects from distance

videos.description: Luka Doncic makes a 3-pointer over Josh Hart for Los Angeles.
videos.headline: Luka flashes a big smile after a tough 3

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Luka flashes a big smile after a tough 3) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: Karl-Anthony Towns cleans up the Jalen Brunson miss with a dunk for the Knicks.
videos.headline: KAT flexes after big dunk

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (KAT flexes after big dunk) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: Luka Doncic gives the Lakers the lead with a 3-pointer, then has some courtside fun with Spike Lee.
videos.headline: Luka winks at Spike Lee after splashing a 3

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Luka winks at Spike Lee after splashing a 3) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: OG Anunoby slams in the assist by Jalen Brunson to pad the Knicks' lead.
videos.headline: Jalen Brunson feeds OG Anunoby for a two-handed flush

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Jalen Brunson feeds OG Anunoby for a two-handed flush) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: OG Anunoby gets up for the beautiful slam dunk
videos.headline: OG Anunoby gets up for the beautiful slam dunk

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (OG Anunoby gets up for the beautiful slam dunk) 2026/02/02 ESHEET

videos.description: Landry Shamet knocks down a pair of 3-pointers for the Knicks down the stretch.
videos.headline: Landry Shamet hits back-to-back 3s to lock up Knicks' win

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Landry Shamet hits back-to-back 3s to lock up Knicks' win) 2026/02/02 ESHEET
